title: stenostomum involucratum
intro: Stenostomum involucratum is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, commonly known as the "wild jasmine" due to its fragrant white flowers that attract pollinators. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its antispasmodic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Stenostomum involucratum include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce inflammation, and help alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
